question: What is Vue 3?
options: A progressive JavaScript framework for building user interfaces
answer: A progressive JavaScript framework for building user interfaces

question: Which API is newly introduced in Vue 3 for component logic?
answer: Composition API

question: What is the purpose of the Composition API in Vue 3?
options: To better organize and reuse logic inside and between components
answer: To better organize and reuse logic inside and between components

question: How do you create a reactive state using the Composition API?
answer: Both A and B

question: What is the purpose of the reactive() function in Vue 3?
options: To make an object reactive so its properties are tracked
answer: To make an object reactive so its properties are tracked

question: How do you define a computed property using Vue 3's Composition API?
options: By directly returning a function in setup()
answer: Using the computed() function

question: What is the role of the setup() function in a Vue 3 component?
options: It is the entry point for Composition API logic and is called before the component is created
It compiles templates into render functions
answer: It is the entry point for Composition API logic and is called before the component is created

question: How can you watch a reactive property or computed value in Vue 3?
answer: Using the watch() function

question: Which directive is used to bind an element's attribute to an expression in Vue 3?
answer: v-bind

question: What is v-model used for in Vue 3?
options: Creating two-way data bindings on form inputs and components
answer: Creating two-way data bindings on form inputs and components

question: How do you conditionally render an element in Vue 3?
answer: Using v-if

question: Which directive is used for rendering lists in Vue 3?
answer: v-for

question: How can you handle user events (like clicks) in Vue 3 templates?
answer: Using v-on

question: What is the shorthand syntax for v-bind in Vue 3?
What is the shorthand syntax for v-on in Vue 3?
How do you define a prop in a Vue 3 component using the Options API?
options: By adding a props property in the component’s options
By using the setup() function exclusively
answer: By adding a props property in the component’s options

question: How can Vue 3 improve performance over Vue 2?
options: Through a rewritten Virtual DOM and optimized reactivity system
By removing support for computed properties
answer: Through a rewritten Virtual DOM and optimized reactivity system

question: What are single-file components (SFCs) in Vue 3?
options: Components defined in a single .vue file that encapsulates template, script, and style
Components written only in JavaScript files
Components that can only be used once per application
Components defined in HTML files only
answer: Components defined in a single .vue file that encapsulates template, script, and style

question: Which tool is commonly used to compile Vue single-file components?
answer: Vue CLI or Vite

question: What is the purpose of the v-slot directive in Vue 3?
options: To define scoped slots for passing dynamic content to child components
To bind data to an element
answer: To define scoped slots for passing dynamic content to child components

question: What is a directive in Vue 3?
options: A special token in the markup that tells the library to do something to a DOM element
A built-in function for data fetching
answer: A special token in the markup that tells the library to do something to a DOM element

question: How do you create a custom directive in Vue 3?
options: By using app.directive() during application setup
answer: By using app.directive() during application setup

question: What is the purpose of the teleport component in Vue 3?
options: To render content into a different part of the DOM outside the component hierarchy
answer: To render content into a different part of the DOM outside the component hierarchy

question: How do you manage global state in a Vue 3 application?
options: By using Vuex or the new Composition API with provide/inject
By using only local component state
By storing data in the browser's local storage exclusively
answer: By using Vuex or the new Composition API with provide/inject

question: What is Vue Router used for?
options: Managing client-side routing for single-page applications
answer: Managing client-side routing for single-page applications

question: How do you define routes using Vue Router?
options: By creating an array of route objects and passing it to createRouter()
By directly editing the index.html file
By using a v-for loop in the template
answer: By creating an array of route objects and passing it to createRouter()

question: What is the purpose of createApp() in Vue 3?
options: To initialize and mount a Vue application
To create a new component instance
answer: To initialize and mount a Vue application

question: How can you share logic between Vue 3 components using the Composition API?
options: By extracting reusable functions (composables) that can be imported in setup()
By creating mixins in the Options API
By duplicating code in each component
answer: By extracting reusable functions (composables) that can be imported in setup()

question: What is the defineComponent() function used for in Vue 3?
options: To provide better type inference and define a component
answer: To provide better type inference and define a component

question: What is the role of watchEffect() in Vue 3?
options: It automatically tracks reactive dependencies and re-runs when they change
answer: It automatically tracks reactive dependencies and re-runs when they change

question: How do you register a global component in Vue 3?
options: By importing it in every file
By declaring it in the template
answer: By using app.component('ComponentName', Component)

question: What are mixins in Vue 3?
options: Reusable pieces of functionality that can be merged into components
A way to store state globally
answer: Reusable pieces of functionality that can be merged into components

question: How can you perform asynchronous operations in Vue 3 components?
options: By using async functions within the setup() function or lifecycle hooks
answer: By using async functions within the setup() function or lifecycle hooks

question: What is the role of lifecycle hooks in Vue 3?
options: They allow you to perform actions at specific stages of a component’s lifecycle
answer: They allow you to perform actions at specific stages of a component’s lifecycle

question: Which lifecycle hook in Vue 3 is equivalent to mounted() in Vue 2?
answer: onMounted()

question: What is the purpose of the provide and inject API in Vue 3?
options: To share data between components without prop drilling
To define reactive state within a component
answer: To share data between components without prop drilling

question: How do you create a computed property using the Options API in Vue 3?
options: By adding a computed property in the component’s options
By using the setup() function exclusively
answer: By adding a computed property in the component’s options

question: What is the benefit of using Single-File Components (SFCs) in Vue 3?
options: They encapsulate template, logic, and styles in one file for better organization
They eliminate the need for CSS
They improve the performance of API calls
answer: They encapsulate template, logic, and styles in one file for better organization

question: What is the purpose of the emit function in Vue 3?
options: To send custom events from a child component to its parent
To update the reactive state directly
To bind props to a component
answer: To send custom events from a child component to its parent

question: How do you listen to an emitted event in a parent component in Vue 3?
options: By using v-on to bind an event handler to the custom event
By calling watch() on the event
answer: By using v-on to bind an event handler to the custom event

question: How do you handle dynamic class binding in Vue 3?
options: Using v-bind:class with an object or array syntax
By hardcoding class names in the template
Using v-for to loop over class names
answer: Using v-bind:class with an object or array syntax

question: How do you bind inline styles dynamically in Vue 3?
options: Using v-bind:style with an object containing camelCased CSS properties
By linking an external stylesheet only
answer: Using v-bind:style with an object containing camelCased CSS properties

question: What is a slot in Vue 3?
options: A placeholder in a component where content can be injected from the parent
answer: A placeholder in a component where content can be injected from the parent

question: How do you define a named slot in a Vue 3 component?
options: Using the v-slot:name syntax in the template
By using the setup() function exclusively
answer: Using the v-slot:name syntax in the template

question: What is the purpose of the keep-alive component in Vue 3?
options: To cache inactive component instances and preserve their state
answer: To cache inactive component instances and preserve their state

question: How do you perform conditional rendering of components in Vue 3?
options: By dynamically changing the component name
answer: Using v-if and v-else directives

question: What is the role of the transition component in Vue 3?
options: To apply transition effects to elements or components when they enter or leave the DOM
answer: To apply transition effects to elements or components when they enter or leave the DOM

question: How do you customize transition classes in Vue 3?
options: By using the name attribute on the transition component
By modifying the Virtual DOM directly
answer: By using the name attribute on the transition component

question: How do you mount a Vue 3 application to a DOM element?
options: By calling app.mount('#app') after creating the app with createApp()
By calling new Vue() and passing a selector
answer: By calling app.mount('#app') after creating the app with createApp()

question: What is the purpose of the emits option in a Vue 3 component?
options: To declare the custom events that a component can emit
To define props for the component
answer: To declare the custom events that a component can emit

question: How do you validate props in Vue 3?
options: By adding a props option with type, required, and default properties
By using v-bind on the template
By calling a custom validator in setup()
Prop validation is not supported in Vue 3
answer: By adding a props option with type, required, and default properties

question: What is a mixin in Vue 3 and how is it used?
options: A reusable piece of code that can be merged into components to share common functionality
A method for dynamic component loading
A function to create reactive state
answer: A reusable piece of code that can be merged into components to share common functionality

question: How can you access the global properties in a Vue 3 application?
options: Global properties are not accessible in Vue 3
answer: Using app.config.globalProperties

question: What is the role of defineAsyncComponent() in Vue 3?
options: To define a component that is loaded asynchronously for code splitting
answer: To define a component that is loaded asynchronously for code splitting

question: How do you handle errors in asynchronous components in Vue 3?
options: By using errorCaptured or a custom error handler on the async component
By using try/catch in the template
By relying on the browser's default error handling
Errors in asynchronous components cannot be handled
answer: By using errorCaptured or a custom error handler on the async component

question: How do you update reactive data in Vue 3?
options: By directly assigning a new value to a ref or property of a reactive object
answer: By directly assigning a new value to a ref or property of a reactive object

question: What is the purpose of shallowReactive() in Vue 3?
options: To create a reactive object that tracks only its root properties and not nested objects
answer: To create a reactive object that tracks only its root properties and not nested objects

question: What is the difference between ref() and reactive() in Vue 3?
options: ref() creates a reactive primitive or object wrapped in a value property, while reactive() makes an entire object reactive
ref() is used only in class components
reactive() cannot be used with objects
answer: ref() creates a reactive primitive or object wrapped in a value property, while reactive() makes an entire object reactive

question: How do you expose component data when using the Composition API?
options: By returning the reactive data and functions from the setup() function
By using the data() option exclusively
By exporting them from the component file
answer: By returning the reactive data and functions from the setup() function

question: How can you integrate third-party libraries with Vue 3?
options: By installing them via npm/yarn and importing them in your components or plugins
By copying their code into your component files
By using inline script tags in the template
Third-party libraries cannot be used with Vue 3
answer: By installing them via npm/yarn and importing them in your components or plugins

question: What is the purpose of Vue DevTools?
options: To inspect and debug Vue component hierarchies, state, and events
answer: To inspect and debug Vue component hierarchies, state, and events

question: How do you define a custom filter in Vue 3?
options: Filters have been deprecated in Vue 3; you should use methods or computed properties instead
By adding a filters object in the component options
answer: Filters have been deprecated in Vue 3; you should use methods or computed properties instead

question: How can you integrate TypeScript in a Vue 3 project?
options: By using Vue CLI or Vite with TypeScript templates and adding type annotations in components
By converting .vue files to .ts files exclusively
TypeScript is not supported in Vue 3
answer: By using Vue CLI or Vite with TypeScript templates and adding type annotations in components

question: What is the purpose of defineProps() in Vue 3 with the Composition API (in SFCs)?
options: To declare and type-check props in a <script setup> block
To create a new reactive object
answer: To declare and type-check props in a <script setup> block

question: What is the role of defineEmits() in Vue 3 with the Composition API?
options: To declare the events that a component can emit in a <script setup> block
answer: To declare the events that a component can emit in a <script setup> block

question: How can you conditionally apply CSS classes in Vue 3?
options: Using v-bind:class with an object or array syntax
By using v-if on the style attribute
By creating separate components for each style
answer: Using v-bind:class with an object or array syntax

question: How do you perform two-way binding on form inputs in Vue 3?
options: Two-way binding is not supported in Vue 3
answer: Using v-model

question: What is the purpose of nextTick() in Vue 3?
options: To execute code after the DOM has been updated
answer: To execute code after the DOM has been updated

question: How do you share reusable logic between components without mixins in Vue 3?
options: By using composable functions (composables) with the Composition API
By duplicating code in every component
answer: By using composable functions (composables) with the Composition API

question: What are portals used for in Vue 3?
options: To render a component’s template in a different part of the DOM
answer: To render a component’s template in a different part of the DOM

question: How do you create a plugin in Vue 3?
options: By exporting an object with an install() method that receives the app instance
By writing a mixin and adding it to every component
By using the plugin() method on the component
Plugins are not supported in Vue 3
answer: By exporting an object with an install() method that receives the app instance

question: What is the role of the Suspense component in Vue 3?
options: To handle asynchronous components and show a fallback while loading
To delay rendering of all components
To provide error boundaries for components
answer: To handle asynchronous components and show a fallback while loading

question: How do you lazy-load a component in Vue 3?
options: By using v-if to conditionally render it
By setting the component's lazy property to true
answer: By using defineAsyncComponent()

question: What is the role of expose() in a Vue 3
options: To expose specific properties or methods to the parent component
answer: To expose specific properties or methods to the parent component

question: How do you conditionally render a component in Vue 3?
options: By modifying the component's reactive state in mounted()
answer: Using v-if and v-else directives

question: What is a watcher in Vue 3?
options: A function that observes changes in reactive data and runs a callback
A method to bind CSS classes
answer: A function that observes changes in reactive data and runs a callback

question: How do you create a deep watcher in Vue 3?
options: By setting the deep option to true in the watch() function
By using reactive() on the watched property
By using a computed property instead
Deep watchers are not supported in Vue 3
answer: By setting the deep option to true in the watch() function

question: How can you improve the performance of a large Vue 3 application?
options: By using lazy loading, code splitting, and optimizing reactive updates
By writing all logic in the template
answer: By using lazy loading, code splitting, and optimizing reactive updates

question: What is the role of the beforeMount lifecycle hook in Vue 3?
options: It runs before the component is mounted to the DOM
It runs after the component is unmounted
It runs during data fetching only
answer: It runs before the component is mounted to the DOM

question: How do you update a reactive array in Vue 3?
options: By using methods like push(), pop(), or by reassigning the array with a new one
By using v-bind on the array
By calling update() on the array
answer: By using methods like push(), pop(), or by reassigning the array with a new one

question: What is the purpose of Vue 3’s new reactivity system based on Proxies?
options: To track changes more efficiently and enable better performance
To replace the need for components
answer: To track changes more efficiently and enable better performance

question: How can you conditionally apply inline styles in Vue 3?

By using the v-if directive on style tags
answer: By binding an object to the style attribute using v-bind:style

question: How do you register a global directive in Vue 3?

By including it in the component's directives option
By using v-directive in the template
answer: By calling app.directive() with the directive name and definition

question: What is the use of the onUnmounted() hook in Vue 3?
options: To perform cleanup operations when a component is unmounted
To fetch data when a component is mounted
answer: To perform cleanup operations when a component is unmounted

question: How do you integrate a Vue 3 component with a REST API?

By binding API calls directly in the template
By using v-model on the API endpoint
answer: By using lifecycle hooks or the setup() function with async/await to fetch data

question: What is the difference between onMounted() and onBeforeMount() in Vue 3?
options: onBeforeMount() runs just before mounting, while onMounted() runs after the component is mounted
They run at the same time
onMounted() is only for reactive state initialization
answer: onBeforeMount() runs just before mounting, while onMounted() runs after the component is mounted

question: How do you use the emit function with TypeScript in Vue 3?

By using v-bind on the event
TypeScript is not supported with emit
answer: By defining the emits option and using defineEmits() in <script setup>

question: What is the purpose of using shallowRef() in Vue 3?
options: To create a ref that tracks only its own value and not nested properties
To create a deep reactive object
answer: To create a ref that tracks only its own value and not nested properties

question: How do you perform cleanup of side effects in Vue 3 when using watch()?
options: By returning a cleanup function from the watch() callback
By calling unwatch() manually inside setup()
Cleanup is automatic and requires no code
answer: By returning a cleanup function from the watch() callback

question: What is the use of markRaw() in Vue 3?
options: To mark an object so that it should not be made reactive
To create a reactive copy of an object
To force reactivity on a primitive value
To perform deep cloning of objects
answer: To mark an object so that it should not be made reactive

question: How do you create a custom composable function in Vue 3?
options: By defining a function that uses reactive APIs (e.g., ref, reactive, watch) and returns the desired properties
By using the computed property exclusively
Custom composables are not supported in Vue 3
answer: By defining a function that uses reactive APIs (e.g., ref, reactive, watch) and returns the desired properties

question: What is the purpose of using toRefs() in Vue 3?

To convert refs into reactive objects
answer: To convert a reactive object’s properties into refs, preserving reactivity when destructuring

question: How do you handle component errors globally in a Vue 3 application?
options: By using the errorHandler option in app.config
By wrapping every component in try/catch blocks
By using v-if to check for errors in the template
answer: By using the errorHandler option in app.config

question: What is the purpose of createSSRApp() in Vue 3?

To automatically hydrate the client app
answer: To create an application instance optimized for server-side rendering

question: How can you share a global mixin in Vue 3?
options: By calling app.mixin() with the mixin object during app initialization
By importing the mixin in every component
By declaring the mixin in the template
Global mixins are not supported in Vue 3
answer: By calling app.mixin() with the mixin object during app initialization

question: How do you integrate CSS frameworks like Tailwind CSS in a Vue 3 project?
options: By installing the framework via npm/yarn and including its CSS in your main entry file
By rewriting all styles as inline styles
By using a Vue-specific version of the framework exclusively
By copying the CSS files into every component
answer: By installing the framework via npm/yarn and including its CSS in your main entry file

question: What is the purpose of the computed property in Vue 3?
options: To cache computed values based on reactive dependencies
answer: To cache computed values based on reactive dependencies

question: How do you bind a component’s class and style dynamically in Vue 3?
options: By using v-bind:class and v-bind:style with objects or arrays
By using inline JavaScript in the template
answer: By using v-bind:class and v-bind:style with objects or arrays

question: What is the role of h() in Vue 3?
options: It is a helper function to create VNodes when writing render functions
answer: It is a helper function to create VNodes when writing render functions

question: What are some best practices for building scalable Vue 3 applications?
options: Using the Composition API for logic reuse, splitting code via lazy loading, and maintaining modular architecture
Writing all code in a single file
Avoiding the use of plugins and external libraries
Hardcoding configuration settings in each component
answer: Using the Composition API for logic reuse, splitting code via lazy loading, and maintaining modular architecture
